content.full_text:
TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[✓] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.
[✓] HELEN   [✓] OTHER John M.

FROM: Jeanne
DATE: 4/27/82

TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: 82015 and 84211
INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [✓]

CALLER: Gary Proctor, Treasurer for
REPRESENTING: Judicial candidate, Orange County
PHONE NUMBER: (714) 547-5853

A number of lawyers each agree to sell a certain number of tickets to a fundraiser, and agree that if they do not sell all the tickets they will make up the difference with a personal contribution. They each give the treasurer a post-dated check in the full amount that would be received if they sell all of the tickets, with the understanding that they will be liable only for that amount over the actual amount received from the sale of the tickets.

QUESTION: Has the committee received "pledges" which must be reported during the period?

ANSWER: Yes. Based on the circumstances of this agreement, the committee received pledges in the amount of the post-dated checks. The individuals may actually pay only a portion of the pledge because a portion may be paid by proceeds from selling the tickets, but nevertheless the committee has received a pledge in the full amount. I advised that he should amend the 1/1-3/17 statement to include the pledges, with an explanation of the circumstances.
